_description: Migration seed — adds missing SEO admin menu items and role permissions. Run the companion SQL below or use the MenusService syncMissingMenus() auto-sync (triggered on backend restart).
sql: INSERT IGNORE INTO menus (name, slug, icon, section, sort_order, is_active, created_at, updated_at) VALUES ('SEO Templates', 'admin_seo_templates', 'zap', 'admin_seo', 149, 1, NOW(), NOW());
INSERT IGNORE INTO menus (name, slug, icon, section, sort_order, is_active, created_at, updated_at) VALUES ('All SEO Pages', 'admin_seo_all_pages', 'file-text', 'admin_seo', 150, 1, NOW(), NOW());
INSERT IGNORE INTO role_menu_permissions (role, menu_id, is_visible) SELECT 'super_admin', id, 1 FROM menus WHERE slug = 'admin_seo_templates';
INSERT IGNORE INTO role_menu_permissions (role, menu_id, is_visible) SELECT 'super_admin', id, 1 FROM menus WHERE slug = 'admin_seo_all_pages';
INSERT IGNORE INTO role_menu_permissions (role, menu_id, is_visible) SELECT 'admin', id, 1 FROM menus WHERE slug = 'admin_seo_templates';
INSERT IGNORE INTO role_menu_permissions (role, menu_id, is_visible) SELECT 'admin', id, 1 FROM menus WHERE slug = 'admin_seo_all_pages';
note: The backend MenusService has syncMissingMenus() and syncMissingPermissions() which run automatically on every server start. Simply restarting the backend will apply these changes without needing to run the SQL manually.
slug_convention: URL slug order is always {prefix}-in-{locality}-{city} when locality is present. City-only: {prefix}-in-{city}. Examples: /property-for-sale-in-sector-56-noida, /agents-in-sector-56-noida. The splitCityLocality parser resolves city from the longest matching suffix in KNOWN_CITY_SLUGS.
